goal: Pilot goal: move 30–50 receipt snaps from phone into YOURCLOUD/To Process/Receipts, OCR and rename, dedupe, and establish a simple capture habit.
Convert existing JPEGs of paper pages into searchable PDFs via OCR during migration.
constraints: Sensitive photos should be kept in an encrypted cloud folder or local encrypted drive.
Avoid CamScanner if privacy is a concern; prefer Microsoft Lens, Adobe Scan, or Scanner Pro.
Prefer client-side encryption or zero-knowledge storage before cloud upload; minimize reliance on mainstream cloud providers for sensitive data.
If cloud is used, ensure end-to-end encryption and manage encryption keys locally.
Do not expose raw SMB/AFP shares to the Internet.
Use NAS as a secure local backup and a sync target for encrypted vaults.
For sensitive documents, keep client-side encryption; Cryptomator vault(s) stored on the NAS share.
Use NAS for automated encrypted backups (restic or rclone+enc) from both machines; keep one encrypted offline copy on a USB drive stored safely.
Prefer VPN (WireGuard/OpenVPN) for remote access rather than exposing NAS services directly.
Tax/financial records must be retained for seven years; implement an auditable retention policy across vaults, backups, and purge processes.
All highly sensitive documents (passports, SSNs) must be encrypted client-side before any off-site copies are made.
Home upload speed is slow; large initial cloud syncs would be frustrating.
decisions: Cryptomator vault(s) on NAS for sensitive documents (and separate vault for everyday docs).
Use restic as the backup tool (to NAS via SFTP or local share) with daily incremental backups and 30-day retention.
Optionally Nextcloud on NAS for phone scanning/sync (if you want integrated path) or keep simple manual upload via Cryptomator.
Phone capture path: choose between Simple (Microsoft Lens/Adobe Scan → Cryptomator) vs Nextcloud-based scanning path.
Adopt a 7-year tax/financial retention policy; configure restic to retain appropriate snapshots and maintain offline encrypted copies.
Enforce client-side encryption for highly sensitive documents before off-site copies; implement Cryptomator vault for passports/SSNs.
OCR step for existing JPEGs: convert to searchable PDF/A during migration using OCRmyPDF (Tesseract).
Automate nightly encrypted backups to NAS using restic or NAS backup target.
entities: Remote access configuration (LAN-only vs internet exposure)
Encrypted vault options on NAS (Cryptomator, VeraCrypt)
Data volume: 1–2 TB (mixed photos and PDFs).
Predominant file types: images and PDFs.
Highly sensitive documents (passports, SSNs) encryption workflow
OCR workflow for existing JPEGs (JPEGs→PDFs via OCRmyPDF)
open: Await confirmation on archival format policy: adopt PDF/A master + TIFF image-only and define per-type scanning defaults (dpi, color, etc).
Phone photo chaos: define intake path and automation to move receipts to YOURCLOUD/To Process/Receipts; confirm tooling across iOS/Android for scanning and OCR.
Decide primary storage approach given privacy concerns: client-side encryption with mainstream cloud vs zero-knowledge provider vs local NAS.

NAS configuration details: gather brand/model for model-specific checklist, remote access exposure, RAID layout, mounting from Windows/macOS, encrypted vault options on NAS (Cryptomator, VeraCrypt), and backup/snapshot strategy.
Await NAS brand/model for model-specific checklist.
Decide between simple Cryptomator vault + manual upload vs Nextcloud-based scanning path.
Integrate 7-year retention policy into intake, folder structure, and automation.
Implement policy to encrypt passports/SSNs client-side before off-site copies and adjust intake flow accordingly.
Define OCR workflow for existing JPEGs: tool choice, DPI targets, language packs, output naming, and storage path; validate end-to-end before migration.
Implement automated nightly encrypted backups to NAS (restic or NAS backup target) and establish monthly offline backup to USB.
